source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 16.3.2-16.4 journald.conf & coexistence (pp. 319-322)

questions.q: Which journald.conf Storage= value makes the journal PERSISTENT (kept in /var/log/journal/)?
questions.why: Storage=persistent stores logs in /var/log/journal/ (you may need to create it); volatile keeps them in RAM (/run), none saves nothing, auto behaves like persistent if the dir exists.

questions.q: What is the DEFAULT value of Storage= in journald.conf?
questions.why: The default is auto (which is similar to persistent - it uses /var/log/journal if that directory exists).

options: Stores the journal only in memory (the /run/log/journal/ temporary files)
questions.why: volatile keeps logs in memory under /run/log/journal/, so they are lost on reboot.

questions.q: What does ForwardToSyslog= control, and what is its default?
options: Whether journald forwards messages to the traditional syslog daemon; default is no
Whether to compress logs; default yes
Whether to store logs; default auto
Whether to rotate logs; default weekly
questions.why: ForwardToSyslog=no by default; set it yes to feed a classic syslog daemon.

questions.q: By default, can rsyslog and journald coexist?
options: Yes - they coexist without affecting each other (rsyslog reads the journal via the imjournal input module)
questions.why: By default both run; rsyslog integrates the journal through the imjournal module rather than the syslog socket.
